title: Construction of EAQECCs with imperfect ebits
abstract: We generalize the stabilizer formalism for entanglement-assisted quantum error-correcting codes with noisy ebits (EAQECCs-Ne) from the binary case to the general $q$-ary case, where $q$ is a prime power. By leveraging the structure of the generalized Pauli group over $\mathbb{F}_q$ and symplectic geometry over $\mathbb{F}_q^{2n}$, we establish a unified framework for constructing EAQECCs-Ne for qudit systems. Equivalent formulations in terms of symplectic geometry over $\mathbb{F}_q$ and additive codes over $\mathbb{F}_q^{2n}$ are derived. We further construct several families of $q$-ary EAQECCs with noise ebits and analyze their performance compared to optimal stabilizer codes. Our results demonstrate that under certain noise conditions, the proposed EAQECCs-Ne can outperform standard stabilizer codes with equivalent error-correcting capability, offering a promising approach for fault-tolerant quantum computation in high-dimensional quantum systems.
summary: This paper extends the theory of entanglement-assisted quantum error correction to higher-dimensional qudit systems and incorporates the realistic scenario of noisy shared entanglement. It proves that if the shared entanglement is well-protected, these new codes can provide better error protection than standard stabilizer codes.
main_problem: Generalizing the stabilizer formalism for Entanglement-Assisted Quantum Error-Correcting Codes (EAQECCs) from the binary case to the q-ary case while accounting for noise in the shared ebits.
main_result: The authors established a unified framework for constructing q-ary EAQECCs with noisy ebits (EAQECCs-Ne) and demonstrated that these codes can outperform standard stabilizer codes when ebit noise is sufficiently low.
method: The study utilizes symplectic geometry over finite fields, the generalized Pauli group, and additive codes over quadratic extension fields to derive equivalent formulations and explicit code constructions.
model_or_system: A quantum communication model involving a sender (Alice) and a receiver (Bob) using q-ary qudit systems, where both the transmitted qudits and the shared entanglement (ebits) are subject to depolarizing noise.
important_parameters: q-ary dimension (q), code parameters [n, k, d; c], depolarizing rates for qudits (pa) and ebits (pb), and the entanglement degradation coefficient (lambda = pb/pa).
main_assumptions: Assumes the existence of pre-existing entanglement between parties and relies on the mathematical properties of finite fields where q is a prime power.
figures_summary: Figure 1 provides a comparative analysis of channel fidelity versus the depolarizing rate pa under four different scenarios of ebit noise levels (ranging from pb = 0.01pa to pb = 0.99pa).
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of noisy ebits, develops a generalized stabilizer formalism using symplectic geometry and additive codes, provides explicit construction methods for new q-ary EAQECCs-Ne, and concludes with a performance comparison against standard stabilizer codes.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: Fig. 1 Comparative analysis of the Channel fidelity under four scenarios. We use the depolarizing rate pa and the Channel fidelity as the X and Y axes, respectively.
